headlines.title: Houthis Force Saudi Oil Tankers To U-Turn in Red Sea
headlines.summary: Shipping disruption: Houthi threats forced two Saudi oil tankers to turn around in the Red Sea as Hormuz traffic slowed sharply after more tanker attacks. headlines.title: IMF Flags Higher Oil Price As Key Risk to India’s GDP Growth
headlines.summary: India’s economic growth for the 2026/2027 fiscal year could be lower than previously expected amid higher oil prices with the re-escalation of the Middle East war and the El Nino weather phenomenon, a senior official at the International Monetary Fund (IMF) told Reuters. “The dow

headlines.title: Ryanair Profit Falls 36% as Unhedged Jet Fuel Costs Double
headlines.summary: Ryanair shed more than a third of its profit as rising jet fuel prices triggered by the Iran war began to take effect. The budget airline had previously insulated itself from rising fuel prices by fixing its energy costs on hedged contracts. But Ryanair said the cost of the 20 pe

headlines.title: Oil Prices Top $90 as Kuwaiti Tanker Hit in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: A Kuwait-owned oil products tanker was struck by a projectile in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman as tensions escalate around the key shipping lanes of the Middle East, sending Brent back above $90 per barrel. The Kaifan tanker, owned by Kuwait Oil Tanker Co. S.A.K., was the vessel

headlines.title: India Keeps Buying Russian Oil at Near-Record Pace Despite Expired Waiver
headlines.summary: India's crude oil imports have remained close to record-high levels in July despite the end of the U.S. waiver the previous month. The U.S. quietly let the waiver allowing the purchase of Russian oil loaded on tankers expire on June 17, just as the U.S. and Iran signed the memora

headlines.title: Pakistan Shells Out Record Sums for Spot LNG as Qatar Supply Falters
headlines.summary: Pakistan is paying top dollar for LNG supply as the renewed closure of the Strait of Hormuz and the stranding of cargoes from its term supplier Qatar have forced the South Asian country to seek liquefied natural gas on the spot market. headlines.title: Oil Reverses Gains on Renewed Hope of Peace in Iran
headlines.summary: Crude oil prices reversed their climb this morning, retreating on reports that efforts are being made to negotiate another ceasefire between the United States and Iran. headlines.title: Goldman Warns Oil Could Hit $120 as Middle East War Drags On
headlines.summary: Crude oil prices could surge to $120 per barrel towards the end of the year if the war in the Middle East extends and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed for longer, Goldman Sachs has warned in its latest prediction in response to geopolitical developments in the Middle East. headlines.title: Higher-For-Longer Oil Scenario Is Here To Stay
headlines.summary: Oil prices have been on a climb for over a week now as hostilities in the Middle East continue, and despite recent talk of a glut, the physical market is flashing signs of tightness—and higher oil for longer.
